item.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago with star-studded celebrations
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.

item.title: Former first lady Michelle Obama praises her husband in presidential center dedication speech
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it "an honor" to be by her husband's side. Watch her full speech.

item.title: Michelle Obama's speech at center opening moves husband to tears
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama delivered a loving and heartfelt tribute to her husband at the Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony in Chicago Thursday.

item.title: GOP congressman, missing for months, set to return on June 30
item.description: Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr. of New Jersey will return to Congress on June 30, his spokesperson said, after being away since March in an unexplained absence that has confounded Capitol Hill.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic ramps up in wake of U.S.-Iran deal signing
item.description: More tankers are transiting the Strait of Hormuz after 111 days of gridlock, but Iran and the U.S. still have much to discuss.

item.title: The Fed just paused interest rates again. Here are 3 affordable ways you can still borrow money.
item.description: The Fed held rates steady again this week, but a few borrowing options are still cheaper than you may expect.

item.title: Watch: Obama speaks at dedication of presidential center in Chicago
item.description: Former President Barack Obama's new presidential center in Chicago held its grand opening on Thursday. Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is."

item.title: NYC celebrates Knicks NBA championship with ticker-tape parade
item.description: The New York Knicks went up New York City's famous Canyon of Heroes for a massive ticker-tape parade celebrating the 2026 NBA champions.

item.title: Tornadoes tear through multiple states across Midwest, Southeast
item.description: Tornadoes were reported in Illinois, Iowa and several other states Wednesday as severe weather slammed a large swath of the Midwest​ and Southeast.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Texas man who challenged case on drug use, gun ownership
item.description: The Supreme Court unanimously found that a Texas man's prosecution for having a firearm while he was an occasional, unlawful marijuana user was too broad an interpretation of the law and violated his Second Amendment. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: How to find the highest CD interest rate this June
item.description: Interest rates on CDs will stay high after this week's Fed meeting. Here's how to find the highest ones right now.

item.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis after some vehicles entered construction zone
item.description: The recall follows multiple incidents in which Waymo robotaxis drove past ramp-closure signs and into freeway construction zones.

item.title: Less than half of Americans can afford quality healthcare, Gallup finds
item.description: Gallup found that only 49% of Americans were "cost-secure" last year, with concerns about medical bills and prescription costs rising across income groups.

item.title: Hegseth announces review of U.S. forces in Europe, blasts "shameful" NATO allies
item.description: The Pentagon is launching a six-month review of U.S. forces and bases in Europe, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday at a meeting of NATO defense ministers.

item.title: Americans say they're reading fewer books. Here's what they're doing instead.
item.description: Many people are spending more time on screens, but also doing more physical activities, a new CBS News poll finds.

item.title: Mamdani delivers passionate Knicks victory speech to "a city overcome by happiness"
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani spoke to an overflowing crowd of passionate fans gathered to celebrate the Knicks' NBA Championship title. "The Knicks did not just win for New York City, they won like New York City," Mamdani said. "For 53 years we waited. Now, we've won."

item.title: Watch: Vance makes case for Iran agreement, reacts to Trump saying he would blame him if deal breaks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance held a press briefing at the White House on Thursday and provided more details on the timing of the Iran deal after President Trump signed the memorandum of understanding with Tehran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Republicans, Democrats blast Trump's U.S.-Iran agreement
item.description: Republicans and Democrats are voicing their concerns over the Trump administration's agreement with Iran. Samantha Vinograd, a former assistant secretary for counterterrorism at the Department of Homeland Security, shares her analysis.

item.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
item.description: Republican Sens. Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis and Ted Cruz have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.

item.title: Can debt collectors pursue surviving spouses for unpaid debt?
item.description: When a spouse dies with debt, creditors could still come calling. Here's what surviving spouses should know.

item.title: French Montana helps cab driver after his taxi was vandalized following Knicks win
item.description: French Montana told "CBS Mornings" the taxi driver's story "hit a nerve" for him and explained how he's helping to assist him.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with man who challenged law barring drug users from having guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.

item.title: "Lady Gucci," who had her ex-husband killed, could soon inherit $23M fortune
item.description: Despite spending 18 years in prison for orchestrating the murder of her ex-husband, fashion heir Maurizio Gucci, Patrizia Reggiani may be about to inherit a fortune.

item.title: Photos show celebrations at Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

item.title: Gas prices dip below $4 a gallon on average for first time in months after Iran deal progress
item.description: Gas prices have dropped in reaction to President Trump signing a memorandum of understanding with Iran -- but they're still a dollar higher than they were before the war, according to AAA data. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger explains how markets are reacting to global relations.

item.title: Destructive tornado "sounded like thunder" as it hurled tree into Illinois home, witness says
item.description: At least seven tornadoes descended on parts of the Midwest and Southeast Wednesday night, ripping through homes and buildings and leaving a trail of destruction in their wake. CBS News' Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: U.S. plans expansion of denaturalization push, aiming for 250 cases by fall
item.description: The Trump administration expects to try to revoke the U.S. citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by October, a Justice Department official said.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against government in gun case involving drug use
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with a Texas man and ruled against the government after its prosecution of a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: This law firm has deep ties to Trump. Now a partner is his pick for a top job.
item.description: Jamie McDonald, a partner at the firm Sullivan & Cromwell, is President Trump's choice to helm one of the nation's most prestigious federal prosecutors' offices.

item.title: Explore the Obama Presidential Center and Library opening in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.
